{{Quote|Think of peace and honor – and act upon that only!|A Tyia student, name unknown|<ref name="Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', Second Edition]]</ref>}}
The '''Tyia''' was the religion of the [[Revwien/Legends|Revwiens]], a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Plant/Legends|plant]]-like [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Revyia]], who worshiped the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and taught of holding a high regard for peace and life. The Tyia was predominantly practiced by the Revwiens, to whom its ideals were very appealing.

==History==
While some sources claimed that the religion came to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] as late as [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], Revwien Thuwistens had been seen across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for much of its history. Whenever they could, the Thuwistens spread the word of their beliefs to any who would listen.

At one point, [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] studied their methods in his quest to gain full mastery of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the Tyia masters on Revyia were highly disturbed by the deaths of so many [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] during the execution of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] at the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], which they could sense through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The beginning of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] prompted them to call a meeting within a [[Revyia temple|temple]] grown from the largest [[Tree/Legends|trees]] on their world. They then charged their most capable students with seeking out any surviving Jedi and aiding them in whatever way possible.<ref name="JATM" /> [[Unidentified Revwien Tyia adept|One such]] Revwien Tyia adept was able to seek out and consult with a Human Jedi.

==Philosophy==
Tyia was named after the Revwien word for "breath". They viewed the Force as being in everything that breathed. When things were moving in accord with the Force, the Tyia practitioners called this "Harmony" which was akin the the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]. When the Dark side of the Force was dominant, the Tyia would be in Discord and the adepts would attempt to bring it back to Harmony.<ref name="JATM" />

The followers of Tyia believed that conflict was part of life but did their best to cause no harm and often used [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] to stun enemies rather than slaying them.<ref name="JATM" />

Tyia was a religion highly in tune with the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]. It taught that the value of peace and the sanctity of all life should be held above anything else. Its followers were expected to think and act honorably.

Tyia practitioners were known as [[Thuwisten]], taking their name from the Tyia's prime tenet that one should seek "personal harmony with one's own Tyia so that other Tyia forces may join to form a bond-circle, a joining of spirits called 'Thuwisten', complete in their utter harmony." Indeed, Thuwisten believed that individual introspection was key to knowing the ways of the universe.

Tyia-practicing Force users were taught to control their personal Force through ceremony and meditation. Their focus on their inner Force resulted in Thuwisten having a heightened awareness of the skill the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] knew as "Control"; a Thuwisten found the use of the "Alter" ability to be a more difficult and time-consuming practice than the Jedi. Nonetheless, many Thuwisten regarded the Jedi as true masters of the Tyia.
